page: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton processors, specifically the Neoverse-N1 model. It features 32 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz with a single thread per core, ensuring dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and a bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed temporary storage. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 12 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results show average performance across most multi-threaded and memory-intensive tasks. The instance demonstrates strong performance in large-block read-only memory bandwidth, reaching 173.34 GB/sec. Multi-core CPU performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48571.1 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11706.0. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 958.0 and lower single-threaded compression and decompression metrics. Database operations are stable, with Redis SET operations achieving 40.61 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers competitive compute density and resource efficiency for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 alternatives, though it lacks advanced vector and matrix extensions like AVX or AMX. The combination of dedicated physical cores and local NVMe SSD storage makes this instance well-suited for distributed databases, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates strong large-block read memory bandwidth at 173.34 GB/sec, alongside average multi-core CPU and database performance, though single-core performance remains a relative tradeoff. Lacking advanced vector extensions, this instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads. It is highly suitable for applications such as distributed databases, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong large-block memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and high-speed local NVMe storage for multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory and 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 173.34 GB/sec
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for distributed databases, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving
